name: Turkey Enchiladas with Sour Cream
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Fresh tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and chopped
3 cups Shredded cooked turkey or chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove and discard the seeds from the chiles and chop the chiles finely.
2. In a 10-12-inch skillet, sauté the chiles with the garlic in 2 tablespoons oil over medium-high heat, stirring often. Add the tomatoes, onions, 1 teaspoon salt, oregano, and water. Simmer uncovered until thick, stirring often for about 30 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl combine the turkey, sour cream, grated cheese, and remaining 1 teaspoon salt; mix well.
5. Heat the 1/3 cup oil in a heavy 8-10-inch skillet. Cook each tortilla briefly in oil, turning once. Place each tortilla between paper towels to remove excess oil.
6. Fill each tortilla with about 4 tablespoons of the turkey filling. Roll up and place seam side down in a shallow 8 x 12-inch baking dish. Repeat with additional tortillas if needed.
7. Pour the sauce over the top of the enchiladas. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven until heated through, about 20 minutes. If preparing ahead, bake for 25-30 minutes when held refrigerated.
8. Serve with refried beans and beer.
